questions.name: Architecture
questions.question_text: A maintainable front-end usually separates concerns by organizing data, rendering, and interaction logic.

answers.text: Start with the richest animation possible
Start with meaningful content and add richer behavior when supported
answers.html: Start with meaningful content and add richer behavior when supported

answers.text: Hide content until all APIs return
Specific, timely, and easy to recover from
answers.html: Specific, timely, and easy to recover from

answers.text: A readable way to coordinate promises

questions.name: API design
questions.question_text: When rendering remote data, a robust interface should handle loading, success, and error states.

questions.name: Storage choice
questions.question_text: Which storage option is best for a small saved theme preference?

answers.text: To hide all code from teammates
To organize and reuse code with clearer boundaries
answers.html: To organize and reuse code with clearer boundaries

questions.name: Components
questions.question_text: A component-based framework is most useful when you need to:

answers.text: Repeat structured UI patterns with state and behavior
answers.html: Repeat structured UI patterns with state and behavior

answers.text: Not all users interact with a mouse
answers.html: Not all users interact with a mouse

questions.name: Deployment review
questions.question_text: After deployment, which task is still necessary?

answers.text: Verify links, layout, and interactions in the live environment
answers.html: Verify links, layout, and interactions in the live environment

questions.name: Portfolio readiness
questions.question_text: A strong final presentation should explain audience, problem, decisions, and evidence of quality.

questions.name: async-state-machine
questions.question_text: A fetch function renders new content but the loading indicator never disappears after the request completes, even when it succeeds. Where should the code that hides the loading indicator be placed?

answers.text: In a finally block — finally runs whether the request succeeds or fails, ensuring the loading state is always cleared
answers.html: In a finally block — finally runs whether the request succeeds or fails, ensuring the loading state is always cleared

questions.name: module-dependency-direction
questions.question_text: In a modular Vite project, api.js contains a function that calls document.getElementById('results'). What design rule does this violate?

answers.text: api.js should have no DOM access — it is responsible for network requests only; DOM manipulation belongs in render.js
answers.html: api.js should have no DOM access — it is responsible for network requests only; DOM manipulation belongs in render.js

answers.text: api.js functions must be async, and getElementById is synchronous
answers.html: api.js functions must be async, and getElementById is synchronous

answers.text: getElementById can only be called from main.js
answers.html: getElementById can only be called from main.js

answers.text: This is acceptable as long as api.js does not also use localStorage
answers.html: This is acceptable as long as api.js does not also use localStorage

questions.name: vue-props-direction
questions.question_text: A child Vue component needs to tell its parent that a button was clicked. Which pattern should the component use?

answers.text: Emit a custom event with defineEmits and $emit — data flows up via events, not by directly modifying the parent's data
answers.html: Emit a custom event with defineEmits and $emit — data flows up via events, not by directly modifying the parent's data

answers.text: Directly modify the prop passed from the parent
answers.html: Directly modify the prop passed from the parent

answers.text: Use localStorage to communicate between components
Call a function on the parent component using document.querySelector
answers.html: Call a function on the parent component using document.querySelector

questions.name: gitignore-purpose
questions.question_text: Which files should be listed in a .gitignore for a Vite/Node.js project, and why?

answers.text: node_modules/ and dist/ — they can be rebuilt from package.json and source files, so committing them wastes space and creates merge conflicts
answers.html: node_modules/ and dist/ — they can be rebuilt from package.json and source files, so committing them wastes space and creates merge conflicts

answers.text: index.html and style.css — HTML and CSS should never be committed
answers.html: index.html and style.css — HTML and CSS should never be committed

answers.text: All JavaScript files — only configuration files belong in git
answers.html: All JavaScript files — only configuration files belong in git

answers.text: The .gitignore file itself — it is generated automatically on push
answers.html: The .gitignore file itself — it is generated automatically on push

questions.name: lighthouse-render-blocking
questions.question_text: A Lighthouse audit flags a page's First Contentful Paint as 'poor.' The page includes <script src="app.js"> in <head> without any attributes. Which change would most directly improve this score?

answers.text: Add defer — the browser can then parse HTML and download the script in parallel, unblocking the initial render
answers.html: Add defer — the browser can then parse HTML and download the script in parallel, unblocking the initial render

answers.text: Move the script tag to the bottom of body and remove defer
answers.html: Move the script tag to the bottom of <body> and remove defer

answers.text: Add more CSS custom properties to reduce JavaScript size
answers.html: Add more CSS custom properties to reduce JavaScript size

answers.text: Replace all arrow functions with regular function declarations
answers.html: Replace all arrow functions with regular function declarations
